note: Explorer Latest Transactions section now lazy-loads older history. Previously the table only showed the last 30 blocks plus up to 200 transfers — once the user clicked OLDER through to the local OLDEST, there was no way to keep walking back. Now the OLDER button auto-fetches the next 30 blocks via getblockhash + getblock when the local pager hits its last page; subsequent clicks keep walking back until block 0. Button label tracks state: 'OLDER →' inside the cached dataset, 'LOAD OLDER →' when the next click triggers a backend fetch, 'LOADING…' (disabled) while the batch is in flight, 'GENESIS REACHED' (disabled) once block 0 is included. Auto-refresh (every 30 s) only re-scans the last 30-block head and PRESERVES the older rows the user already pulled in — page 3+ no longer wipes on each tick. The 'X of Y transactions' header now shows '· loading older blocks…' while a batch is in flight. NO consensus, NO RPC schema, NO node, NO miner. Pure explorer-side feature; uses only existing getblockhash / getblock / gettransaction RPCs.
